translation: When all his army was annihilated and the remaining soldiers fled away, the extremely mighty Jarāsandha, alone in his chariot confronted Balarāma, the wielder of the plough (as a weapon).

devanagari: पित्रे मगधराजाय जरासन्धाय दुःखिते । वेदयां चक्रतुः सर्वमात्मवैधव्यकारणम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: pitre magadharājāya jarāsandhāya duḥkhite vedayāṃ cakratuḥ sarvamātmavaidhavyakāraṇam
translation: Both of them were enormously big like mountains, proud like intoxicated elephants, fired with indignation and possessed of great strength. They fought with each other.

devanagari: स तदप्रियमाकर्ण्य शोकामर्षयुतो नृप । अयादवीं महीं कर्तुं चक्रे परममुद्यमम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tadapriyamākarṇya śokāmarṣayuto nṛpa ayādavīṃ mahīṃ kartuṃ cakre paramamudyamam

translation: Jarāsandha who was overwhelmed with wrath, took up his heavy mace (Gadā) and turning it round a hundred times, hurled it at the chest of Balarāma.

devanagari: अक्षौहिणीभिर्विंशत्या तिसृभिश्चापि संवृतः । यदुराजधानीं मथुरां न्यरुधत्सर्वतो दिशम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: akṣauhiṇībhirviṃśatyā tisṛbhiścāpi saṃvṛtaḥ yadurājadhānīṃ mathurāṃ nyarudhatsarvato diśam

translation: Dodging the onward dashing gadā, big and heavy like a mountain peak, Balarāma, the wielḍer of a plough (as his weapon) smilingly made his attempt fruitless.

devanagari: निरीक्ष्य तद्बलं कृष्ण उद्वेलमिव सागरम् । स्वपुरं तेन संरुद्धं स्वजनं च भयाकुलम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: nirīkṣya tadbalaṃ kṛṣṇa udvelamiva sāgaram svapuraṃ tena saṃruddhaṃ svajanaṃ ca bhayākulam
translation: Having rendered the mace ineffective by striking it with his pestle, the wrathful Balarāma struck it against the chariot and killed his charioteer.

devanagari: चिन्तयामास भगवान्हरिः कारणमानुषः । तद्देशकालानुगुणं स्वावतारप्रयोजनम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: cintayāmāsa bhagavānhariḥ kāraṇamānuṣaḥ taddeśakālānuguṇaṃ svāvatāraprayojanam

translation: Thereupon Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, took out five terrible arrows. With four of them he hit the horses and with one the charioteer, quickly.

devanagari: हनिष्यामि बलं ह्येतद्भुवि भारं समाहितम् । मागधेन समानीतं वश्यानां सर्वभूभुजाम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: haniṣyāmi balaṃ hyetadbhuvi bhāraṃ samāhitam māgadhena samānītaṃ vaśyānāṃ sarvabhūbhujām

translation: He quickly set ten arrows on the bow and discharged them at Kṛṣṇa, the slayer of the demon Madhu. He gave out a roar like a lion and all this happened as if like a miracle.

devanagari: अक्षौहिणीभिः सङ्ख्यातं भटाश्वरथकुञ्जरैः । मागधस्तु न हन्तव्यो भूयः कर्ता बलोद्यमम् ॥ ८ ॥

translation: At that (Śiśupāla’s exploit) Kṛṣṇa applauded him, crying out, “Well ḍone! Bravo! Oh Śiśupāla, you are a great archer. You are a hero and a powerful one at that.

devanagari: एतदर्थोऽवतारोऽयं भूभारहरणाय मे । संरक्षणाय साधूनां कृतोऽन्येषां वधाय च ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: etadartho saṃrakṣaṇāya sādhūnāṃ kṛto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: Now you wicked-minded fellow, see my expertness in archery by experiencing it personally.” While Kṛṣṇa was speaking thus, all the kings simultaneously discharged and hit Kṛṣṇa with sharp arrows. Kṛṣṇa hit each of them with five shafts, dispatching the charioteer and four horses of each chariot to the abode of the god of Death. Kṛṣṇa, the delighter of the Yadus, pierced each of the kings with three arrows, on the chest in the mouth and on the forehead, in the battle. The valiant leader of the Yadus quickly cut down the thirty bows of the thirty kings with thirty shafts in that battle. He laughingly cut off the bow even of Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, and with three shafts cut ḍown the flag, his crown and the umbrella. Thereupon Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Madhu, fitted ten Bhalla type of arrows on his Śārṅga bow. He drew the bowstrings up to the ear and hit Śiśupāla with them in the battle. The army of the kṣatriyas which was pierced with arrows became confounded.

devanagari: अन्योऽपि धर्मरक्षायै देहः संभ्रियते मया । विरामायाप्यधर्मस्य काले प्रभवतः क्वचित् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: anyo virāmāyāpyadharmasya kāle prabhavataḥ kvacit
translation: Now you wicked-minded fellow, see my expertness in archery by experiencing it personally.” While Kṛṣṇa was speaking thus, all the kings simultaneously discharged and hit Kṛṣṇa with sharp arrows. Kṛṣṇa hit each of them with five shafts, dispatching the charioteer and four horses of each chariot to the abode of the god of Death. Kṛṣṇa, the delighter of the Yadus, pierced each of the kings with three arrows, on the chest in the mouth and on the forehead, in the battle. The valiant leader of the Yadus quickly cut down the thirty bows of the thirty kings with thirty shafts in that battle. He laughingly cut off the bow even of Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, and with three shafts cut ḍown the flag, his crown and the umbrella. Thereupon Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Madhu, fitted ten Bhalla type of arrows on his Śārṅga bow. He drew the bowstrings up to the ear and hit Śiśupāla with them in the battle. The army of the kṣatriyas which was pierced with arrows became confounded.

devanagari: एवं ध्यायति गोविन्द आकाशात्सूर्यवर्चसौ । रथावुपस्थितौ सद्यः ससूतौ सपरिच्छदौ ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ dhyāyati govinda ākāśātsūryavarcasau rathāvupasthitau sadyaḥ sasūtau saparicchadau
translation: Now you wicked-minded fellow, see my expertness in archery by experiencing it personally.” While Kṛṣṇa was speaking thus, all the kings simultaneously discharged and hit Kṛṣṇa with sharp arrows. Kṛṣṇa hit each of them with five shafts, dispatching the charioteer and four horses of each chariot to the abode of the god of Death. Kṛṣṇa, the delighter of the Yadus, pierced each of the kings with three arrows, on the chest in the mouth and on the forehead, in the battle. The valiant leader of the Yadus quickly cut down the thirty bows of the thirty kings with thirty shafts in that battle. He laughingly cut off the bow even of Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, and with three shafts cut ḍown the flag, his crown and the umbrella. Thereupon Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Madhu, fitted ten Bhalla type of arrows on his Śārṅga bow. He drew the bowstrings up to the ear and hit Śiśupāla with them in the battle. The army of the kṣatriyas which was pierced with arrows became confounded.

devanagari: आयुधानि च दिव्यानि पुराणानि यदृच्छया । दृष्ट्वा तानि हृषीकेशः सङ्कर्षणमथाब्रवीत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: āyudhāni ca divyāni purāṇāni yadṛcchayā dṛṣṭvā tāni hṛṣīkeśaḥ saṅkarṣaṇamathābravīt
translation: Now you wicked-minded fellow, see my expertness in archery by experiencing it personally.” While Kṛṣṇa was speaking thus, all the kings simultaneously discharged and hit Kṛṣṇa with sharp arrows. Kṛṣṇa hit each of them with five shafts, dispatching the charioteer and four horses of each chariot to the abode of the god of Death. Kṛṣṇa, the delighter of the Yadus, pierced each of the kings with three arrows, on the chest in the mouth and on the forehead, in the battle. The valiant leader of the Yadus quickly cut down the thirty bows of the thirty kings with thirty shafts in that battle. He laughingly cut off the bow even of Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, and with three shafts cut ḍown the flag, his crown and the umbrella. Thereupon Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Madhu, fitted ten Bhalla type of arrows on his Śārṅga bow. He drew the bowstrings up to the ear and hit Śiśupāla with them in the battle. The army of the kṣatriyas which was pierced with arrows became confounded.

devanagari: पश्यार्य व्यसनं प्राप्तं यदूनां त्वावतां प्रभो । एष ते रथ आयातो दयितान्यायुधानि च ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: paśyārya vyasanaṃ prāptaṃ yadūnāṃ tvāvatāṃ prabho eṣa te ratha āyāto dayitānyāyudhāni ca

Kṛṣṇa, the delighter of the Yadus, pierced each of the kings with three arrows, on the chest in the mouth and on the forehead, in the battle. The valiant leader of the Yadus quickly cut down the thirty bows of the thirty kings with thirty shafts in that battle. He laughingly cut off the bow even of Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, and with three shafts cut ḍown the flag, his crown and the umbrella. Kṛṣṇa hit each of them with five shafts, dispatching the charioteer and four horses of each chariot to the abode of the god of Death. Kṛṣṇa, the delighter of the Yadus, pierced each of the kings with three arrows, on the chest in the mouth and on the forehead, in the battle. The valiant leader of the Yadus quickly cut down the thirty bows of the thirty kings with thirty shafts in that battle. He laughingly cut off the bow even of Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, and with three shafts cut ḍown the flag, his crown and the umbrella. Thereupon Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Madhu, fitted ten Bhalla type of arrows on his Śārṅga bow. He drew the bowstrings up to the ear and hit Śiśupāla with them in the battle. The army of the kṣatriyas which was pierced with arrows became confounded.

translation: 17 Keeping all the while the Magadhan army in view, Kṛṣṇa of ferocious valour, continued discharging arrows, blowing his conch and twanging his bow incessantly and moving it in a circle like a fire-brand, in the battle.

devanagari: शङ्खं दध्मौ विनिर्गत्य हरिर्दारुकसारथिः । ततोऽभूत्परसैन्यानां हृदि वित्रासवेपथुः ॥ १६ ॥

translation: Surrounded by a small force, both of them kṛṣṇa and Balarāma equipped themselves with their beautiful weapons and went out of the city. Hari with Dāruka as his charioteer came out of the city and blew conch.

devanagari: तावाह मागधो वीक्ष्य हे कृष्ण पुरुषाधम । न त्वया योद्धुमिच्छामि बालेनैकेन लज्जया । गुप्तेन हि त्वया मन्द न योत्स्ये याहि बन्धुहन् ॥ १७ ...
english_devnagari: tāvāha māgadho vīkṣya he kṛṣṇa puruṣādhama na tvayā yoddhumicchāmi bālenaikena lajjayā guptena hi tvayā manda na yotsye yāhi bandhuhan
translation: It (the challenging conch-blowing) sent a cold shivering of shudder in the hearts of the armies of the enemy. Seeing them both, Jarāsandha said: ‘Oh Kṛṣṇa! Meanest of all men’ (also ‘to whom other men are inferior’, ‘the best among men’).

devanagari: तव राम यदि श्रद्धा युध्यस्व धैर्यमुद्वह । हित्वा वा मच्छरैश्छिन्नं देहं स्वर्याहि मां जहि ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tava rāma yadi śraddhā yudhyasva dhairyamudvaha hitvā vā maccharaiśchinnaṃ dehaṃ svaryāhi māṃ jahi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative middle second single], [imperative passive second single]

translation: [1] I do not wish to fight with a young boy like you as you are alone. It is shameful (to fight with you). I won’t fight with you who lay concealed so long (out of Kaṃsa’s fear) you stupid fellow! The murderer of relatives! You go away (Fighting is not your profession) as you are a gupta (a man of Vaiś’ya community, as you are the son of the cowherd Nanda.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Verse 18.1. Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives a complimentary interpretation of the line:
gupteṇa hi tvayā'manda na yotsye yāhī bandhuhan /
With you who, being the Inner controller of all beings, are beyond the ken of sight. Oh highly intelligent person! You are the destroyer of Avidyā! Be pleased to come (let me attain to you).

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । न वै शूरा विकत्थन्ते दर्शयन्त्येव पौरुषम् । न गृह्णीमो वचो राजन्नातुरस्य मुमूर्षतः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca na vai śūrā vikatthante darśayantyeva pauruṣam na gṛhṇīmo vaco rājannāturasya mumūrṣataḥ

translation: Oh Balarāma! If you have faith and confidence in you, muster some courage and fight with me. Go to heaven leaving your body shattered with my arrows or kill me (if you dare).

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । जरासुतस्तावभिसृत्य माधवौ महाबलौघेन बलीयसाऽवृणोत् । ससैन्ययानध्वजवाजिसारथी सूर्यानलौ वायुरिवाभ्ररेणुभिः ॥ २० ॥

translation:
The glorious Lord replied:
“Those who are really brave do not boast but display their valour. We do not take seriously the words of those who are delirious (or of unbalanced mind through grief due to the death of your son-in-law) or who are on the point of death (as you are now).”

devanagari: सुपर्णतालध्वजचिह्नितौ रथाव् । अलक्षयन्त्यो हरिरामयोर्मृधे । स्त्रियः पुराट्टालकहर्म्यगोपुरं । समाश्रिताः सम्मुमुहुः शुचार्दितः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: suparṇatāladhvajacihnitau rathāv alakṣayantyo harirāmayormṛdhe striyaḥ purāṭṭālakaharmyagopuraṃ samāśritāḥ sammumuhuḥ śucārditaḥ
translation: Jarāsandha, the foster-son of the demoness Jarā, assailed both Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa, the descendants of Madhu, and surrounded them both, their detachment of soldiers, vehicles (chariots), flags, horses, charioteers with a big formidable army, just as a tempestuous wind would cover the sun with clouds or the fire with particles of dust.

devanagari: हरिः परानीकपयोमुचां मुहुः शिलीमुखात्युल्बणवर्षपीडितम् । स्वसैन्यमालोक्य सुरासुरार्चितं व्यस्फूर्जयच्छार्ङ्गशरासनोत्तमम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: hariḥ parānīkapayomucāṃ muhuḥ śilīmukhātyulbaṇavarṣapīḍitam svasainyamālokya surāsurārcitaṃ vyasphūrjayacchārṅgaśarāsanottamam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pīḍ class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ pīḍ class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]
[nominative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]

translation: Finding that the chariots of Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma which were distinguished by the ensigns with the figures of Garuḍa and Palmyra tree had become invisible in that battle, the women of Mathurā who occupied seats on the top of the ramparts, mansions and towers were struck with grief and fell in swoon.

devanagari: गृह्णन्निशङ्गादथ सन्दधच्छरान् । विकृष्य मुञ्चन्शितबाणपूगान् । निघ्नन्रथान्कुञ्जरवाजिपत्तीन् । निरन्तरं यद्वदलातचक्रम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: gṛhṇanniśaṅgādatha sandadhaccharān vikṛṣya muñcanśitabāṇapūgān nighnanrathānkuñjaravājipattīn nirantaraṃ yadvadalātacakram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ grah class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ grah class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ grah class 9 verb]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active second plural]
[nominative single from √ rā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ rā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ muc class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ muc class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ muñc class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ muñc class 1 verb]
[vocative single from √ śā class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ śā class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ śā class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ śā class 4 verb]

translation: Perceiving that his own army was sorely afflicted by incessant showers of extremely sharp arrows from the cloudlike inimical forces, Hari twanged His excellent bow called Śārṅga which was held in high esteem (lit. adored) by both gods and demons alike.

devanagari: निर्भिन्नकुम्भाः करिणो निपेतुरनेकशोऽश्वाः शरवृक्णकन्धराः । रथा हताश्वध्वजसूतनायकाः पदायतश्छिन्नभुजोरुकन्धराः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: nirbhinnakumbhāḥ kariṇo nipeturanekaśo rathā hatāśvadhvajasūtanāyakāḥ padāyataśchinnabhujorukandharāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ tur class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ su class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ su class 2 verb]

translation: Now you wicked-minded fellow, see my expertness in archery by experiencing it personally.” While Kṛṣṇa was speaking thus, all the kings simultaneously discharged and hit Kṛṣṇa with sharp arrows. Kṛṣṇa hit each of them with five shafts, dispatching the charioteer and four horses of each chariot to the abode of the god of Death. Kṛṣṇa, the delighter of the Yadus, pierced each of the kings with three arrows, on the chest in the mouth and on the forehead, in the battle. The valiant leader of the Yadus quickly cut down the thirty bows of the thirty kings with thirty shafts in that battle. He laughingly cut off the bow even of Śiśupāla, the son of Damaghoṣa, and with three shafts cut ḍown the flag, his crown and the umbrella. Thereupon Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Madhu, fitted ten Bhalla type of arrows on his Śārṅga bow. He drew the bowstrings up to the ear and hit Śiśupāla with them in the battle. The army of the kṣatriyas which was pierced with arrows became confounded.

devanagari: सञ्छिद्यमानद्विपदेभवाजिनामङ्गप्रसूताः शतशोऽसृगापगाः । भुजाहयः पूरुषशीर्षकच्छपा हतद्विपद्वीपहय ग्रहाकुलाः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: sañchidyamānadvipadebhavājināmaṅgaprasūtāḥ śataśo bhujāhayaḥ pūruṣaśīrṣakacchapā hatadvipadvīpahaya grahākulāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]
[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: The elephants fell down with their temples split up; a number of horses with their necks cut off with arrows fell down. The chariots stood with the horses, charioteer and the warrior killed and flag staff broken. The foot-soldiers had their arms, thighs and necks cut down.

devanagari: करोरुमीना नरकेशशैवला धनुस्तरङ्गायुधगुल्मसङ्कुलाः । अच्छूरिकावर्तभयानका महा मणिप्रवेकाभरणाश्मशर्कराः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: karorumīnā narakeśaśaivalā dhanustaraṅgāyudhagulmasaṅkulāḥ acchūrikāvartabhayānakā mahā maṇipravekābharaṇāśmaśarkarāḥ
translation: There flowed forth hundreds of streams of blood issued from the shattered bodies of soldiers, elephants and horses which were teeming with (floating acquatic) serpent-like (hacked down) arms, rolling tortoise-like human heads, they were studded with islets of elephant-bodies and were infested with crocodiles in the from of (dead) horses.

devanagari: प्रवर्तिता भीरुभयावहा मृधे मनस्विनां हर्षकरीः परस्परम् । विनिघ्नतारीन्मुषलेन दुर्मदान्सङ्कर्षणेनापरीमेयतेजसा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: pravartitā bhīrubhayāvahā mṛdhe manasvināṃ harṣakarīḥ parasparam vinighnatārīnmuṣalena durmadānsaṅkarṣaṇenāparīmeyatejasā
translation: Therein were hands and thighs of men like fishes, the locks of human hair like moss, bows like ripbles and weapons like bushes. The shields (or wheels.) were like terrible whirl-pools and ornaments of big and costly precious stones formed the stones and sand in them.

devanagari: बलं तदङ्गार्णवदुर्गभैरवं दुरन्तपारं मगधेन्द्र पालितम् । क्षयं प्रणीतं वसुदेवपुत्रयोर्विक्रीडितं तज्जगदीशयोः परम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: balaṃ tadaṅgārṇavadurgabhairavaṃ durantapāraṃ magadhendra pālitam kṣayaṃ praṇītaṃ vasudevaputrayorvikrīḍitaṃ tajjagadīśayoḥ param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pāl class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ pāl class 10 verb]
[nominative single from √ pā ], [accusative single from √ pā ]

translation: By Balarāma of infinite prowess (who was) knocking down with his pestle the proud and powerful enemies in that battle, there started streams of blood which struck terror in the hearts of the cowards and inspired matrial delight and courage in the valiants.

devanagari: स्थित्युद्भवान्तं भुवनत्रयस्य यः । समीहितेऽनन्तगुणः स्वलीलया । न तस्य चित्रं परपक्षनिग्रहस् । तथापि मर्त्यानुविधस्य वर्ण्यते ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: sthityudbhavāntaṃ bhuvanatrayasya yaḥ samīhite na tasya citraṃ parapakṣanigrahas tathāpi martyānuvidhasya varṇyate
translation: Oh dear Parīkṣit? That army impassable and terrible like an ocean of vast limitless (unfordable) expanse and directed (lit. protected) by Jarāsandha was completely annihilated. This was a supreme sportive feat of the sons of Vasudeva who were the Lords of the world.

devanagari: जग्राह विरथं रामो जरासन्धं महाबलम् । हतानीकावशिष्टासुं सिंहः सिंहमिवौजसा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: jagrāha virathaṃ rāmo jarāsandhaṃ mahābalam hatānīkāvaśiṣṭāsuṃ siṃhaḥ siṃhamivaujasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having rendered the mace ineffective by striking it with his pestle, the wrathful Balarāma struck it against the chariot and killed his charioteer.

devanagari: बध्यमानं हतारातिं पाशैर्वारुणमानुषैः । वारयामास गोविन्दस्तेन कार्यचिकीर्षया ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: badhyamānaṃ hatārātiṃ pāśairvāruṇamānuṣaiḥ vārayāmāsa govindastena kāryacikīrṣayā

translation: Like a lion powerfully seizing another lion, Balarāma took Jarāsandha a captive who, though extremely powerful himself, had lost his chariot and the whole of the army and was left alone alive.

devanagari: सा मुक्तो लोकनाथाभ्यां व्रीडितो वीरसम्मतः । तपसे कृतसङ्कल्पो वारितः पथि राजभिः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sā mukto lokanāthābhyāṃ vrīḍito vīrasammataḥ tapase kṛtasaṅkalpo vāritaḥ pathi rājabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vrīḍ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vrīḍ class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ vrīḍ class 10 verb]

translation: With a view to achieve His purpose of (destroying demonic forces which would be mobilised by Jarāsandha) he forbade Jarāsandha’s being bound with the nooses of Varuṇa and cords of human beings (by Balarāma) although Jarāsandha had put to death many enemies (in the past).

devanagari: वाक्यैः पवित्रार्थपदैर्नयनैः प्राकृतैरपि । स्वकर्मबन्धप्राप्तोऽयं यदुभिस्ते पराभवः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: vākyaiḥ pavitrārthapadairnayanaiḥ prākṛtairapi svakarmabandhaprāpto

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental plural from √ vac class 3 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ vac class 2 verb], [instrumental plural from √ vac class 3 verb]

translation: Released and allowed to go by Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, the Protectors of the world, Jarāsandha who was highly respected by warriors, felt ashamed. He made up his mind (to retire from politics and) to perform penance. But on his way (to the forest) he was dissuaded by (his friendly) kings with expressions conveying the import of sacred texts (and quotations from Śāstras) as well as popular maxims and words of worldly wisdom. They convinced him by the argument:) “Your defeat by the Yadus is the consequence of the bondage of your karma (actions in past life)”.

devanagari: हतेषु सर्वानीकेषु नृपो बार्हद्रथस्तदा । उपेक्षितो भगवता मगधान्दुर्मना ययौ ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: hateṣu sarvānīkeṣu nṛpo bārhadrathastadā upekṣito bhagavatā magadhāndurmanā yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[locative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Released and allowed to go by Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, the Protectors of the world, Jarāsandha who was highly respected by warriors, felt ashamed. He made up his mind (to retire from politics and) to perform penance. But on his way (to the forest) he was dissuaded by (his friendly) kings with expressions conveying the import of sacred texts (and quotations from Śāstras) as well as popular maxims and words of worldly wisdom. They convinced him by the argument:) “Your defeat by the Yadus is the consequence of the bondage of your karma (actions in past life)”.

devanagari: मुकुन्दोऽप्यक्षतबलो निस्तीर्णारिबलार्णवः । विकीर्यमाणः कुसुमैस्त्रीदशैरनुमोदितः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: mukundo vikīryamāṇaḥ kusumaistrīdaśairanumoditaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ īr class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ īr ]

translation: With all his army annihilated and himself slighted by the Lord by showing indifference to him, king Jarāsandha, the son of Bṛhad-ratha returned to Magadha with his heart weighed with despair and melancholy.

devanagari: माथुरैरुपसङ्गम्य विज्वरैर्मुदितात्मभिः । उपगीयमानविजयः सूतमागधबन्दिभिः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: māthurairupasaṅgamya vijvarairmuditātmabhiḥ upagīyamānavijayaḥ sūtamāgadhabandibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ su class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ su class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ su class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ su class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Kṛṣṇa also whose army was unscathed (as the wounds of his soldiers immediately healed with his nectarine glance and who had crossed over the ocean of the inimical forces (by crushing them), was applauded (with words “Well done! Bravo”) and was showered over with flowers by gods.

devanagari: शङ्खदुन्दुभयो नेदुर्भेरीतूर्याण्यनेकशः । वीणावेणुमृदङ्गानि पुरं प्रविशति प्रभौ ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhadundubhayo nedurbherītūryāṇyanekaśaḥ vīṇāveṇumṛdaṅgāni puraṃ praviśati prabhau
translation: He returned to his capital while his victory was being celebrated in songs by the congregations of the citizens of Mathurā and bards like Sūta, Māgadha and Bandins whose hearts were delighted with the total disappearance of the feverish panic (caused by the siege of Mathurā).

devanagari: सिक्तमार्गां हृष्टजनां पताकाभिरभ्यलङ्कृताम् । निर्घुष्टां ब्रह्मघोषेण कौतुकाबद्धतोरणाम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: siktamārgāṃ hṛṣṭajanāṃ patākābhirabhyalaṅkṛtām nirghuṣṭāṃ brahmaghoṣeṇa kautukābaddhatoraṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hṛṣ class 4 verb]

translation: As the Lord entered the capital, conches, drums, kettledrums, trumpets were loudly and incessantly sounded and lutes, flutes and mṛdaṅgas were every now and then played upon.

devanagari: निचीयमानो नारीभिर्माल्यदध्यक्षताङ्कुरैः । निरीक्ष्यमाणः सस्नेहं प्रीत्युत्कलितलोचनैः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: nicīyamāno nārībhirmālyadadhyakṣatāṅkuraiḥ nirīkṣyamāṇaḥ sasnehaṃ prītyutkalitalocanaiḥ
translation: The roads of the city were sprinkled with water. The population was jubilant. The city was decorated with flags and was resonant with the recitation of the Vedas. Ornamental arches with festoons suspended were erected all over the way. (By way of worship and reception) Kṛṣṇa was showered over with flowers and tender shoots by women; curds mixed with unbroken grains of rice were applied to his forehead and he was gazed with affection by the people with their loving eyes full of joy.

devanagari: आयोधनगतं वित्तमनन्तं वीरभूषणम् । यदुराजाय तत्सर्वमाहृतं प्रादिशत्प्रभुः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: āyodhanagataṃ vittamanantaṃ vīrabhūṣaṇam yadurājāya tatsarvamāhṛtaṃ prādiśatprabhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: The roads of the city were sprinkled with water. The population was jubilant. The city was decorated with flags and was resonant with the recitation of the Vedas. Ornamental arches with festoons suspended were erected all over the way. (By way of worship and reception) Kṛṣṇa was showered over with flowers and tender shoots by women; curds mixed with unbroken grains of rice were applied to his forehead and he was gazed with affection by the people with their loving eyes full of joy.

devanagari: एवं सप्तदशकृत्वस्तावत्यक्षौहिणीबलः । युयुधे मागधो राजा यदुभिः कृष्णपालितैः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ saptadaśakṛtvastāvatyakṣauhiṇībalaḥ yuyudhe māgadho rājā yadubhiḥ kṛṣṇapālitaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The Lord (Kṛṣṇa) presented to Ugrasena, the king of Yadus, all the spoils of the battle, the untold treasure (left behind by the flying army) and the ornaments and jewelry of the warriors (killed and hence) lying on the battle field.{GL_NOTE:191058:

devanagari: अक्षिण्वंस्तद्बलं सर्वं वृष्णयः कृष्णतेजसा । हतेषु स्वेष्वनीकेषु त्यक्तोऽगादरिभिर्नृपः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: akṣiṇvaṃstadbalaṃ sarvaṃ vṛṣṇayaḥ kṛṣṇatejasā hateṣu sveṣvanīkeṣu tyakto

[locative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: In this manner Jarāsandha, king of Magaḍhas mobilised every time an army of the same (twenty-three) number of Akṣauhiṇīs and fought for seventeen times (in succession) with the Yadus who were protected by Lord Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: अष्टादशम सङ्ग्राम आगामिनि तदन्तरा । नारदप्रेषितो वीरो यवनः प्रत्यदृश्यत ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: aṣṭādaśama saṅgrāma āgāmini tadantarā nāradapreṣito vīro yavanaḥ pratyadṛśyata
translation: (In every expedition) the Vṛṣṇis completely destroyed the whole of his army through the dint of Kṛṣṇa’s spiritual power. With his forces completely slaughtered and himself allowed to escape by the enemies, king Jarāsandha returned to his capital.

devanagari: रुरोध मथुरामेत्य तिसृभिर्म्लेच्छकोटिभिः । नृलोके चाप्रतिद्वन्द्वो वृष्णीन्श्रुत्वात्मसम्मितान् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: rurodha mathurāmetya tisṛbhirmlecchakoṭibhiḥ nṛloke cāpratidvandvo vṛṣṇīnśrutvātmasammitān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: While the eighteenth invasion was imminent, there appeared in the interval, a Yavana hero (Kālayavana) who was despatched by Nārada.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
According to Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5.23.6 it was in reply to the query of Kālayavana as to who is a match to him that Nārada mentioned the name of Yādavas. Nārada did not instigate him against Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: तं दृष्ट्वाचिन्तयत्कृष्णः सङ्कर्षण सहायवान् । अहो यदूनां वृजिनं प्राप्तं ह्युभयतो महत् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ dṛṣṭvācintayatkṛṣṇaḥ saṅkarṣaṇa sahāyavān aho yadūnāṃ vṛjinaṃ prāptaṃ hyubhayato mahat
translation: Kālayavana (who regarded himself as) the matchless hero in this world, heard (the report from Nārada) that Vṛṣṇis were his equals in war. He invaded and laid siege to Mathurā with three crores of Mlecchas(barbarians).

devanagari: यवनोऽयं निरुन्धेऽस्मानद्य तावन्महाबलः । मागधोऽप्यद्य वा श्वो वा परश्वो वागमिष्यति ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: yavano māgadho
translation: Seeing him (with that vast army), Kṛṣṇa who had Balarāma as His companion, conferred (with him), “Alas! A great calamity is befalling Yadus on both sides (from Kālayavana and Jarāsandha. Today this Yavana with his vast army has completely beleaguered us. Jarāsandha, the king of Magadha also will be coming today, tomorrow or day after tomorrow.

devanagari: आवयोः युध्यतोरस्य यद्यागन्ता जरासुतः । बन्धून्हनिष्यत्यथ वा नेष्यते स्वपुरं बली ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: āvayoḥ yudhyatorasya yadyāgantā jarāsutaḥ bandhūnhaniṣyatyatha vā neṣyate svapuraṃ balī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative single from √ han class 1 verb], [locative single from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ han class 1 verb], [locative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ han class 2 verb], [locative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Seeing him (with that vast army), Kṛṣṇa who had Balarāma as His companion, conferred (with him), “Alas! A great calamity is befalling Yadus on both sides (from Kālayavana and Jarāsandha. Today this Yavana with his vast army has completely beleaguered us. Jarāsandha, the king of Magadha also will be coming today, tomorrow or day after tomorrow.

devanagari: तस्मादद्य विधास्यामो दुर्गं द्विपददुर्गमम् । तत्र ज्ञातीन्समाधाय यवनं घातयामहे ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādadya vidhāsyāmo durgaṃ dvipadadurgamam tatra jñātīnsamādhāya yavanaṃ ghātayāmahe
translation: If Jarāsandha, the son of Jarā, comes while both of us are engaged in fighting with this (Kālayavana), the mighty Magadhan king will massacre our clansmen or take them captive to his capital.

devanagari: इति सम्मन्त्र्य भगवान्दुर्गं द्वादशयोजनम् । अन्तःसमुद्रे नगरं कृत्स्नाद्भुतमचीकरत् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti sammantrya bhagavāndurgaṃ dvādaśayojanam antaḥsamudre nagaraṃ kṛtsnādbhutamacīkarat
translation: Therefore, let us construct today a fortress inaccessible to human beings (lit. bipeds) and keeping our kinsmen safe in that fort, we shall arrange for the killing of this Kālayavana.”[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
As per Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5.23.1-4 Gārgya who was insulted by Yadus got a boon from Lord Śiva that his son (Kālayavana) would terrorise Yadus and would be invincible. To respect Śiva’s boon, Kṛṣṇa did not kill him.

devanagari: दृश्यते यत्र हि त्वाष्ट्रं विज्ञानं शिल्पनैपुणम् । रथ्याचत्वरवीथीभिर्यथावास्तु विनिर्मितम् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: dṛśyate yatra hi tvāṣṭraṃ vijñānaṃ śilpanaipuṇam rathyācatvaravīthībhiryathāvāstu vinirmitam
translation: Having thus consulted with Balarāma, the Lord caused a fortress constructed in the (western) sea. In the fortress, he got built a city twelve yojanas (96 miles) in area and wonderful in every respect.

devanagari: सुरद्रुमलतोद्यान विचित्रोपवनान्वितम् । हेमशृङ्गैर्दिविस्पृग्भिः स्फटिकाट्टालगोपुरैः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: suradrumalatodyāna vicitropavanānvitam hemaśṛṅgairdivispṛgbhiḥ sphaṭikāṭṭālagopuraiḥ
translation: The building of the city exhibited the expertise in architecture and the skill in masonry of Tvaṣṭṛ, the architect of gods. The roads, quadrangles, streets (and residential areas) were constructed strictly in conformity to the prescribed tenets of the science of architecture (pertaining to city building).

devanagari: राजतारकुटैः कोष्ठैर्हेमकुम्भैरलङ्कृतैः । रत्नकूतैर्गृहैर्हेमैर्महामारकतस्थलैः ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: rājatārakuṭaiḥ koṣṭhairhemakumbhairalaṅkṛtaiḥ ratnakūtairgṛhairhemairmahāmārakatasthalaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[instrumental plural from √ kū class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ kū class 2 verb], [instrumental plural from √ kū class 6 verb], [instrumental plural from √ kū class 9 verb]
[instrumental plural from √ kū class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ kū class 2 verb], [instrumental plural from √ kū class 6 verb], [instrumental plural from √ kū class 9 verb]

translation: In that city, gardens planted with celestial trees and creepers and wonderful parks were laid out. It was built with sky-scraping, gold-towered buildings and balconies of crystals.

devanagari: वास्तोष्पतीनां च गृहैर्वल्लभीभिश्च निर्मितम् । चातुर्वर्ण्यजनाकीर्णं यदुदेवगृहोल्लसत् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: vāstoṣpatīnāṃ ca gṛhairvallabhībhiśca nirmitam cāturvarṇyajanākīrṇaṃ yadudevagṛhollasat
translation: It had barns built of silver and brass which were adorned with gold pitchers. The houses therein were of gold with jewelled domes and floors paved with gold and big emeralds.

devanagari: सुधर्मां पारिजातं च महेन्द्रः प्राहिणोद्धरेः । यत्र चावस्थितो मर्त्यो मर्त्यधर्मैर्न युज्यते ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: sudharmāṃ pārijātaṃ ca mahendraḥ prāhiṇoddhareḥ yatra cāvasthito martyo martyadharmairna yujyate
translation: It was provided with shrines with upper storeys dedicated to gods. It was thickly populated with persons belonging to the four classes of the society. It appeared beautiful with the palaces of the rulers of the Yadu clan.

devanagari: श्यामैकवर्णान्वरुणो हयान्शुक्लान्मनोजवान् । अष्टौ निधिपतिः कोशान्लोकपालो निजोदयान् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: śyāmaikavarṇānvaruṇo hayānśuklānmanojavān aṣṭau nidhipatiḥ kośānlokapālo nijodayān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ aś class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ aś class 5 verb]

translation:
For the sake of Hari, the great Indra transported his own assembly-hall Sudharmā and the celestial tree Pārijāta[5] resorting to which a human being is not subjected to the conditions (such as hunger, thirst, old age, death) affecting a mortal.
[Padaratnāvalī’s text adds:]
55(A). This city became a beloved resort of Lord Viṣṇu (Kṛṣṇa), the God of gods who resides on the milky ocean.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā explains this anachronism by stating that although Pārijāta was not brought at the time of building Dvārakā, the event that Kṛṣṇa brought it from Indra took place before this narration of the Bhāgavata by Śuka.

devanagari: यद्यद्भगवता दत्तमाधिपत्यं स्वसिद्धये । सर्वं प्रत्यर्पयामासुर्हरौ भूमिगते नृप ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: yadyadbhagavatā dattamādhipatyaṃ svasiddhaye sarvaṃ pratyarpayāmāsurharau bhūmigate nṛpa
translation:
Varuṇa gifted (snow-) white horses with one black ear, swift like mind in speed.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text]
56(A). The Lord of waters presented to the God of gods one thousand such horses. Kubera, the presiding deity over treasures gave him eight treasures[6], while Guardian deities of the worlds submitted their special type of wealth to him.
56(B). Similarly, guardian deities of other spheres presented him the wealth special to them.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
S.R. quotes the following list of eight treasures:
padmas'caiva mahāpadmo matsya-kūrmau tathaudakaḥ /
nīlo mukundaḥ śaṅkhaś ca nidhayo'ṣṭau prakīrtitāḥ //

devanagari: तत्र योगप्रभावेन नीत्वा सर्वजनं हरिः । प्रजापालेन रामेण कृष्णः समनुमन्त्रितः । निर्जगाम पुरद्वारात्पद्ममाली निरायुधः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra yogaprabhāvena nītvā sarvajanaṃ hariḥ prajāpālena rāmeṇa kṛṣṇaḥ samanumantritaḥ nirjagāma puradvārātpadmamālī nirāyudhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sam class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: When Lord Hari incarnated on the earth, Siddhas to whom all powers were given for the execution of their duties by the Lord, resubmitted all the powers etc, to him.
